stories.title: **Headline:** Murakami Surges to Sole MLB HR Lead at 13 — On 65-Homer Pace

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Munetaka Murakami just passed Aaron Judge and Yordan Alvarez to take SOLE possession of the MLB home run lead. Murakami: 13 HRs. Judge: 12. Alvarez: 12. The White Sox rookie is on pace for 65 home runs. This is historic. #WhiteSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Murakami's first 12 MLB extra-base hits were ALL home runs. A record going back to at least 1900. The previous record was 10, set by Dae-ho Lee in 2016. Now he's at 13 HRs and leading the league alone. The White Sox may have found something special. #WhiteSox

article.headline: Murakami Surges to Sole MLB HR Lead at 13 — White Sox Rookie on Historic Pace

stories.title: **Headline:** Vargas Extends Historic Streak to 27 Games — Breaks Venezuela-Born Record

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Ildemaro Vargas just broke the MLB record for Venezuela-born players with a 27-game hitting streak. His 24-game season-opening run is the 2nd longest since 1940. He leads the NL in batting average at .378. One of the best stories in baseball right now. #Dbacks #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Vargas is now 3 hits from matching Ron LeFlore's 30-game season-opening streak from 1976 — one of the most obscure records in baseball. The D-backs second baseman leads the NL at .378 BA. Arizona is 6-2 with four home runs last night. #Dbacks #MLB

article.headline: Ildemaro Vargas Extends Streak to 27 Games — Breaks Venezuela Record, Chases LeFlore's 1976 Mark

stories.title: **Headline:** Judge + Rice = Yankees' Modern Mantle-Berra — Two at 10+ HRs Before Game 30

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Aaron Judge (12 HR) + Ben Rice (10 HR) = the first Yankees duo since Mickey Mantle and Yogi Berra in 1956 to both reach 10 HRs before game 30. New York is 19-10 and leading the AL East. The Bronx is back. #Yankees #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Braves Lead Baseball with 6.5-Game Division Lead Entering May — First to 20 Wins

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The Atlanta Braves (20-9) enter May with a 6.5-game lead — tied for the 3rd-largest division lead entering May since 1969. They were the first team to 20 wins. Best ERA AND most runs in baseball. They look like the real deal. #Braves #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** NL Central — All Five Teams Still Above .500 Entering May, Historic Race

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: ALL FIVE NL Central teams are above .500 entering May. Only the second time in the last 90 years for a full division to reach this point still all winning. Three playoff spots. Five contenders. Something historic may be happening in the NL Central. #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Jordan Walker is MLB's Biggest Surprise — Cardinals 23-Year-Old Arriving in 2026

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text: Jordan Walker, 23, was a Top 5 prospect in 2023. Then posted a negative bWAR three years in a row. In 2026: .547 SLG, 8 HR, 19 RBI in 28 games. League execs voted him MLB's "biggest surprise." What a turnaround story. #Cardinals #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** May Milestones Watch — Five Numbers That Could Rewrite the 2026 MLB Record Book

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text: Five numbers to chase in May: Murakami is on a 65-HR pace (Bonds record: 73). Vargas is 3 hits from LeFlore's 1976 streak record. Judge needs ~20 HRs for 400 career. Ramirez chasing rare 3rd straight 30-30. NL Central could make history. #MLB
